explicit_text: I was born in Syria in 1971 and grew up there with my family. Life in Syria was hard, especially since my father passed away when I was very young. We lived in a farming village before moving to Aleppo in 1988 to seek a better life. The war in Syria forced me and my family to leave, and it deeply affected us as we lost our home and were separated from many relatives.
implicit_text: I was born in the country in 1971 and grew up there with my family. Life was hard, especially since my father passed away when I was very young. We lived in a farming village before moving to a major northern city in 1988 to seek a better life. The war forced me and my family to leave, and it deeply affected us as we lost our home and were separated from many relatives.

explicit_text: I grew up in Aleppo city after moving there from the village in 1988. In Aleppo, I worked in a clothing factory starting in 1991, sewing sportswear for brands like Nike and exporting to France. Life was busy and hard, working long hours six days a week. Aleppo was where we tried to build a new life after leaving the village.
implicit_text: I grew up in a major northern city after moving there from the village in 1988. In that city, I worked in a clothing factory starting in 1991, sewing sportswear for well-known brands and exporting to France. Life was busy and hard, working long hours six days a week. That city was where we tried to build a new life after leaving the village.

explicit_text: My brother worked in Lebanon for some time while we were living in Aleppo. It was part of our family's efforts to find better opportunities outside Syria. Lebanon was one of the places my family members went to during those challenging years.
implicit_text: My brother worked in a neighboring Middle Eastern country for some time while we were living in the northern city. It was part of our family's efforts to find better opportunities outside our home country. That country was one of the places my family members went to during those challenging years.

explicit_text: My brother moved to Cyprus, and I visited him there in 1994. That visit was important because I saw his wife not wearing a headscarf, which influenced my decision to stop covering my head. Traveling alone to Cyprus was a big deal culturally for me, but it helped me break from traditional expectations.
implicit_text: My brother moved to an island country in the eastern Mediterranean, and I visited him there in 1994. That visit was important because I saw his wife not wearing a headscarf, which influenced my decision to stop covering my head. Traveling alone to that island was a big deal culturally for me, but it helped me break from traditional expectations.

explicit_text: While working in the clothing factory in Aleppo, we produced sportswear for Nike. It was part of the factory's export business, mainly shipping to France. I started as a tailor and later moved into an administrative role overseeing production lines.
implicit_text: While working in the clothing factory in the northern city, we produced sportswear for a major global athletic brand. It was part of the factory's export business, mainly shipping to France. I started as a tailor and later moved into an administrative role overseeing production lines.

explicit_text: We fled to Malaysia in 2012 because of the war in Syria and stayed there for about five years. Life in Malaysia was difficult since we were illegal refugees without rights to work or free access to services like education and healthcare. Despite the challenges, the people were mostly kind, but the uncertainty and anxiety about resettlement were constant.
implicit_text: We fled to a Southeast Asian country in 2012 because of the war and stayed there for about five years. Life there was difficult since we were illegal refugees without rights to work or free access to services like education and healthcare. Despite the challenges, the people were mostly kind, but the uncertainty and anxiety about resettlement were constant.

explicit_text: In Malaysia, we registered with the United Nations for refugee status and resettlement. They provided us with cultural orientation before moving to the United States, explaining the laws and how to start a new life there. The UN was an important part of our journey to safety and a new home.
implicit_text: In that Southeast Asian country, we registered with an international organization for refugee status and resettlement. They provided us with cultural orientation before moving to the United States, explaining the laws and how to start a new life there. This organization was an important part of our journey to safety and a new home.

explicit_text: Some of my siblings moved to Turkey during the conflict in Syria. It was part of how our family got separated across different countries because of the war. Turkey became a refuge for them while we were in Malaysia and later the United States.
implicit_text: Some of my siblings moved to a neighboring country to the north during the conflict in our home country. It was part of how our family got separated across different countries because of the war. That country became a refuge for them while we were in Southeast Asia and later North America.

explicit_text: One of my brothers relocated to Vienna amidst the turmoil in Syria. This was part of the scattering of my family members across Europe and other parts of the world due to the conflict. Vienna represented a new home for him while we were still displaced.
implicit_text: One of my brothers relocated to the capital of Austria amidst the turmoil in our home country. This was part of the scattering of my family members across Europe and other parts of the world due to the conflict. That city represented a new home for him while we were still displaced.

explicit_text: Many people I knew from Syria, including some friends and acquaintances, resettled in Germany. It became one of the main destinations for Syrian refugees in Europe. I still keep in contact with some people who moved there.
implicit_text: Many people I knew from my home country, including some friends and acquaintances, resettled in a large European country known for its strong economy. It became one of the main destinations for refugees from our region in Europe. I still keep in contact with some people who moved there.

explicit_text: Europe has been a place where many Syrians, including my family and friends, have resettled during the war. Countries like Germany and Austria became new homes for some of my siblings. The diaspora across Europe reflects the widespread impact of the conflict on our community.
implicit_text: The continent has been a place where many from my home country, including my family and friends, have resettled during the war. Countries like the large European nation and Austria became new homes for some of my siblings. The diaspora across this continent reflects the widespread impact of the conflict on our community.

explicit_text: I resettled in the United States in early 2017 after spending years as a refugee in Malaysia. The move was a big change but easier than Malaysia because of the support from organizations like HIAS. The US gave me and my family a chance to start over with help for housing, jobs, and schooling for my children.
implicit_text: I resettled in North America in early 2017 after spending years as a refugee in Southeast Asia. The move was a big change but easier than before because of the support from humanitarian organizations. This country gave me and my family a chance to start over with help for housing, jobs, and schooling for my children.

explicit_text: HIAS was a crucial support organization when I arrived in the United States. They helped us find housing, jobs, furniture, and enrolled my kids in school. Even now, I keep in contact with HIAS whenever I have questions or need assistance. Their help made the transition much smoother for my family.

Even now, I keep in contact with them whenever I have questions or need assistance. Their help made the transition much smoother for my family.

explicit_text: Susan Davis was the interviewer who spoke with me about my life story. She asked about my experiences growing up in Syria, moving through different countries, and resettling in the United States. Our conversations helped me share my journey and reflect on the challenges and hopes I have.
